title: fritillaria alfredae subsp. glaucoviridis
intro: Fritillaria alfredae subsp. glaucoviridis, also known as the Glaucous Green Fritillary, is a rare and exotic bulbous perennial plant native to the Himalayan region. It is a subspecies of the Fritillaria alfredae plant, which is known for its unique glaucous-green leaves and vibrant flowers. The medicinal properties of Fritillaria alfredae subsp. glaucoviridis include its ability to reduce inflammation and treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Fritillaria alfredae subsp. glaucoviridis is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to promote respiratory health, reduce congestion, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
